series_metadata.series_title: STUDENT HEALTH AND MEDICAL CARE RECORDS
series_metadata.series_description: The Code of Alabama 1975 § 6-5-482 requires "all actions against physicians, surgeons, dentists, medical institutions, or other health care providers for liability, error, mistake, or failure to cure, must commence within two years after the act; provided that if the cause of action is not discovered and could not have reasonably been discovered within such period, then the action may commence within six months from the date of such discovery or the discovery of facts which would reasonably lead to such discovery, whichever is earlier; provided further, than in no event may the action commence more than four years after such an act." A period of seven (7) years after the student's last date of attendance covers this statute of limitations on the school's liability for medical treatment.

retention_rules.trigger_desc: Temporary Record. 7 years student's last date of attendance

series_metadata.series_title: DOCUMENTATION OF SELECTION AND PLACEMENT REQUIREMENTS
series_metadata.series_description: These documents provide core documentation of admission criteria and program elements and requirements. They may include admission procedures, eligibility and acceptance guidelines, and other related materials.

retention_rules.trigger_desc: PERMANENT RECORD. in office

series_metadata.series_description: These records document a student's successful matriculation through the Alabama School of Fine Arts. Information contained in these files includes, but is not limited to, identifying data, health/immunization data, standardized test scores, writing assessment results, cumulative card, legal records, final report card, and graduation/ determination data. The requirements for maintaining these records are professional practices that derive from the American Association of Collegiate Registrars and Admissions Officers to ensure standardized record keeping and retention for student records documenting attendance, achievement, and graduation.

retention_rules.trigger_desc: PERMANENT RECORD. in office

series_metadata.series_description: These publications document the school's efforts in communicating with the public regarding information on the activities, academics, and organization of the school. Publications include, but are not limited to, school catalogs, news releases, student handbooks, school yearbooks, student newspapers, promotional brochures and flyers, alumni publications, and school publications directed at parents.

retention_rules.trigger_desc: PERMANENT RECORD

series_metadata.series_description: These records may include scrapbooks, newspaper clippings, photographs, audio/video recordings, and volumes compiling historical information about the school.

retention_rules.trigger_desc: PERMANENT RECORD

series_metadata.series_description: The school maintains a website at: www.asfa.k12.al.us. Information on the website includes school history, organization, admission procedures, news, calendars, alumni association, and school contact data. The website should be preserved as it serves as an important medium for communication with the public.

retention_rules.trigger_desc: PERMANENT RECORD

series_metadata.series_description: The Code of Alabama 1975 § 36-16-8\[1\] requires that "...All \[state agency\] property managers shall keep at all times in their files a copy of all inventories submitted to the Property Inventory Control Division, and the copies shall be subject to examination by any and all state auditors or employees of the Department of Examiners of Public Accounts." These files need to be maintained in the agency's office.

retention_rules.trigger_desc: PERMANENT RECORD. in office

Temporary Record. 1 year. ## Requirements and Recommendations Implementing Records Disposition Authority (RDA) Under Code of Alabama 1975 § 41-13-21, "no state officer or agency head shall cause any state record be destroyed or otherwise disposed of without first obtaining approval of State Records Commission." This Records Disposition Authority constitutes authorization by State Records Commission disposition of records of Alabama School of Fine Arts (hereafter referred as agency) as stipulated in this document. One condition of this authorization is that agency submit an annual Records Disposition Authority (RDA) Implementation Report on agency records management activities, including documentation of records destruction, State Records Commission in April of each year. In addition, agency should make every effort establish and maintain a quality record-keeping program through following activities: - agency should designate a records liaison, who is responsible : ensuring development of quality record keeping systems that meet business and legal needs of agency, coordinating and destruction of records, ensuring that permanent records held on alternative storage media (such as microforms and digital imaging systems) are maintained in compliance with national and state standards, and ensuring regular implementation of agency's approved RDA. - Permanent records in agency's custody should be maintained under proper intellectual control and in an environment that will ensure their physical order and preservation. - Destruction of temporary records, as authorized in this RDA, should occur agency-wide on a regular basis -- example, successful completion of an audit, at end of an administration, or at end of a fiscal year. Despite RDA's provisions, no record should be destroyed that is necessary comply with requirements of state Sunset Act, audit requirements, or any legal notice or subpoena. - agency should maintain full documentation of any computerized record-keeping system it employs. It should develop procedures : (1) backing up all permanent records held in electronic format; (2) storing a back-up copy off-site; and (3) migrating all permanent records when system is upgraded or replaced. If agency chooses maintain permanent records solely in electronic format, it is committed funding any system upgrades and migration strategies necessary ensure records' permanent preservation and accessibility. - Electronic mail contains permanent, temporary, or transitory record information. Although e- mail records can be printed out, filed, and retained according RDA's requirements, agency should preferably employ an electronic records management system capable of sorting e-mail into folders and archiving messages having long-term value. staff of State Records Commission or Examiners of Public Accounts may examine condition of permanent records maintained in custody of agency and inspect records destruction documentation. Government Records Division archivists are available instruct agency staff in RDA implementation and otherwise assist agency in implementing its records management program. State Records Commission adopted this records disposition authority on April 25, 2012. \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ Edwin C.
